signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5715 leads G10 performance as the RBNZ is set to hike rates to 2.50% on July 8. Despite a -150bp nominal carry deficit vs the Fed (3.75%), NZD is the week's top performer while the USD lags following disappointing NFP data. COT LF net short -23,314 (21.6% long) creates a high-conviction short-squeeze regime if the RBNZ delivers the hike, as specs are positioned against the hawkish CB bias. NZD real rate at -0.83% remains accommodative, supporting the case for further tightening.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.27 holds above the critical 160.00 psychological level, increasing the risk of BoJ/MoF intervention. While the +275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, the options market is aggressively hedging downside protection with a 25d RR of -1.67 (strong put skew). COT LF net short -115,033 (28.4% long) shows a crowded carry trade vulnerable to a rapid unwind if intervention occurs or if VIX (15.8) spikes above 25.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Retail Divergence + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4198 exhibits a severe divergence between retail sentiment (93% short) and institutional positioning (COT LF net short -87,096). The pair is decoupling from its traditional energy link with a USD/CAD vs WTI correlation of -0.875 (z = -2.06σ), suggesting the 150bp USD carry advantage and disappointing Canadian data (CESI net -6) are dominating oil price action. Retail capitulation risk is elevated if the pair extends toward 1.43.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Risk Premium Extreme
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6939 shows an extreme Volatility Risk Premium (VRP) of +19.6%, with IV at 26.5% vs HV30 of 6.9%. This indicates the options market is pricing in significant tail-risk protection relative to recent realized movement. Despite the 69bp AONIA-SOFR carry advantage supporting AUD, the 25d RR of -0.78 (put skew) suggests traders are paying up for downside hedges ahead of the August RBA meeting.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Data Outperformance + Carry Neutrality
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3351 is supported by a positive CESI (net +23), reflecting sustained UK data beats against a weakening US data backdrop (USD CESI +282 but slowing). With the Fed-BoE carry spread at 0bp, the pair is driven by growth differentials and real rates (GBP +0.78% vs USD +1.49%). Retail is 72% short, providing a contrarian tailwind for a test of 1.3500 if FOMC minutes confirm a dovish shift.
